ratings.instrument: bank loan facilities (INR4,045cr), NCDs (INR390cr), commercial paper (INR800cr)
ratings.note: Outlook revised to Stable (from prior, presumably Negative/Watch given the language used); ratings reaffirmed: CRISIL AA-/Stable on bank loans and NCDs, CRISIL A1+ on commercial paper. Cites a 'significant turnaround' in Q4 FY26 operating performance, order backlog exceeding INR7,000cr, and the USD1.1bn hyperscaler data-center contract. Net leverage improved to 1.8x from 3.2x; interest coverage improved to 2.9x from 1.9x. Explicitly flags US tariff exposure and the pending Prysmian trade-secret litigation as ongoing risks.

ICRA upgraded long-term ratings from AA- to AA; short-term ratings reaffirmed. Reflects improved financial performance and strong US optical fiber demand, including tailwinds from the US BEAD broadband program.
ratings.source: ICRA Ratings action, 7 Jul 2026 (via press coverage; primary rationale PDF could not be extracted in this run)
